question: Why is water consumption a growing concern for AI data centers?

choices.A: A methodology for continuously monitoring AI model fairness metrics across protected demographic categories
choices.B: AI algorithms run faster when servers are submerged in water
choices.C: A governance protocol for managing access controls and data permissions in AI development environments
choices.D: Data centers use large amounts of water for cooling systems to manage heat generated by GPU clusters

explanation: Data centers ใช้น้ำจำนวนมากใน cooling systems เพื่อระบายความร้อนจาก GPU clusters ที่ train AI models ยิ่ง model ใหญ่ ยิ่งใช้ compute เยอะ ยิ่งร้อน ยิ่งต้องใช้น้ำเยอะ เป็น environmental concern ที่สำคัญขึ้นเรื่อยๆ

question: What is the primary source of energy consumption in AI model training?

choices.A: The computational operations performed by GPUs and TPUs during forward and backward passes
choices.B: The technique of decomposing complex AI tasks into smaller subtasks handled by specialized model components
choices.C: The process of translating business requirements into measurable AI performance objectives and key results
choices.D: A process for evaluating how well AI predictions generalize to unseen scenarios

explanation: Energy consumption หลักของ AI training มาจาก computational operations ที่ GPUs/TPUs ทำระหว่าง forward และ backward passes ซึ่งต้อง run ซ้ำเป็น billions ของ iterations สำหรับ large models ใช้พลังงานมหาศาล

question: What is the primary goal of 'Green AI' initiatives?

choices.A: A technique for calibrating AI confidence scores to match real probabilities
choices.B: To reduce the environmental footprint of AI research and deployment through energy-efficient methods and renewable energy
choices.C: The process of designing AI monitoring alerts that notify teams when model performance falls below defined thresholds
choices.D: Ensuring AI systems achieve maximum computational efficiency while minimizing resource consumption across deployments

explanation: Green AI initiatives มีเป้าหมายลด environmental footprint ของ AI ทั้งด้าน research และ deployment โดยใช้ energy-efficient methods, renewable energy, และ efficient algorithms ไม่ใช่แค่ใช้ AI เพื่อ monitor สิ่งแวดล้อมอย่างเดียว

question: What is carbon-aware computing in the context of AI workloads?

choices.A: A technique for reducing the latency of AI inference in time-critical applications without compromising accuracy
choices.B: An approach to building AI systems that can gracefully handle inputs well outside their trained data distribution
choices.C: Scheduling and routing AI workloads to times and locations where the electricity grid has lower carbon intensity
choices.D: The process of reconciling conflicting outputs from multiple AI models to reach a reliable consensus prediction

explanation: Carbon-aware computing คือการ schedule AI workloads ให้ run ในช่วงเวลาหรือ location ที่ electricity grid มี carbon intensity ต่ำ เช่น ตอนที่มี renewable energy เยอะ ช่วยลด carbon footprint โดยไม่ต้องลด compute

question: Which model architecture technique improves computational efficiency without significantly sacrificing performance?

choices.A: A data preprocessing step that normalizes feature distributions to improve model training convergence speed
choices.B: A technique for mapping the relationships between different AI subsystems in a live production environment
choices.C: Mixture of Experts (MoE), which activates only a subset of model parameters for each input
choices.D: Using only single-precision floating point for all calculations

explanation: Mixture of Experts (MoE) เป็น architecture ที่ activate แค่ subset ของ parameters สำหรับแต่ละ input ทำให้ใช้ compute น้อยลงมากแม้ model จะมี total parameters เยอะ ช่วยให้ efficient ขึ้นโดยไม่เสีย performance มาก

question: What are Small Language Models (SLMs) and why are they gaining attention?

choices.A: Compact language models (typically under 10B parameters) that offer good performance for specific tasks while being cheaper and faster to run
choices.B: A rigorous method for evaluating whether the composition and distribution of AI training datasets adequately represent the full diversity of the intended target population, use cases, and operating conditions
choices.C: The technique of training models on incrementally larger datasets to improve both training stability and output quality
choices.D: Ensuring AI systems achieve maximum computational efficiency while minimizing resource consumption across deployments

explanation: Small Language Models (SLMs) คือ compact models ที่มี parameters น้อย (ต่ำกว่า 10B) แต่ให้ performance ดีสำหรับ specific tasks ข้อดีคือ run ได้ถูกกว่า เร็วกว่า ใช้ energy น้อยกว่า deploy บน edge devices ได้

question: What is a key benefit of on-device AI processing?

choices.A: A framework for categorizing AI system errors by severity and frequency of occurrence
choices.B: A statistical method for measuring the correlation between model inputs and predicted outputs in ML systems
choices.C: A computational approach to simulating the long-term effects of AI deployment on organizational workflows
choices.D: It reduces latency and enhances privacy by processing data locally without sending it to external servers

explanation: On-device AI ลด latency เพราะไม่ต้องส่ง data ไป cloud และเพิ่ม privacy เพราะ data อยู่บน device ไม่ออกไปไหน แถมยังทำงานได้แม้ไม่มี internet แต่มีข้อจำกัดเรื่อง model size ตาม device capacity

question: What do model efficiency benchmarks typically measure?

choices.A: Developing standardized API interfaces that allow different software systems to exchange structured data efficiently
choices.B: The practice of implementing automated data quality checks before inference
choices.C: The process of designing AI monitoring alerts that notify teams when model performance falls below defined thresholds
choices.D: Performance relative to computational cost, including metrics like accuracy-per-FLOP, throughput, latency, and energy consumption

explanation: Model efficiency benchmarks วัด performance เทียบกับ computational cost เช่น accuracy-per-FLOP, throughput, latency, energy consumption ไม่ใช่แค่ accuracy อย่างเดียว เพราะ model ที่แม่นแต่ใช้ resource มหาศาลก็ไม่ถือว่า efficient

question: Which of the following is a sustainable AI practice for organizations?

choices.A: Relying exclusively on post-deployment monitoring to identify and address ethical issues as they emerge in production
choices.B: Tracking and reporting the carbon footprint of AI training runs and choosing appropriately-sized models for each task
choices.C: A framework for measuring AI system uptime and availability across service endpoints
choices.D: Creating comprehensive data catalogs that document available datasets and their metadata across the whole organization

explanation: Sustainable AI practice ที่ดีคือ track carbon footprint ของ training runs และเลือก model size ที่เหมาะกับ task ไม่จำเป็นต้องใช้ largest model เสมอ บางงาน small model ก็ทำได้ดีพอและ ประหยัด energy มากกว่า

question: Why is AI hardware recycling becoming an important environmental issue?

choices.A: Rapid GPU upgrade cycles create large volumes of electronic waste containing hazardous materials and valuable rare metals
choices.B: A method for extracting insights from large volumes of AI training logs
choices.C: A technique for designing AI explanation interfaces that present model reasoning in terms users can readily understand
choices.D: Measuring AI ethics compliance solely through quantitative metrics without incorporating qualitative stakeholder input

explanation: GPU upgrade cycles เร็วมากในยุค AI boom ทำให้เกิด e-waste จำนวนมากที่มี hazardous materials และ rare metals ที่มีค่า การ recycle จึงสำคัญทั้งในแง่ลด pollution และ recover ทรัพยากรที่หายากกลับมา

question: What environmental concern is associated with rare earth minerals used in AI chips?

choices.A: Mining and processing rare earth minerals causes significant environmental damage including toxic waste and habitat destruction
choices.B: A technique for calibrating AI confidence scores to match real probabilities
choices.C: The technique of training models on incrementally larger datasets to improve both training stability and output quality
choices.D: Measuring AI ethics compliance solely through quantitative metrics without incorporating qualitative stakeholder input

explanation: การทำเหมืองและ processing rare earth minerals สร้าง environmental damage มาก ทั้ง toxic waste, water pollution, และทำลาย habitat ซึ่งเป็น hidden environmental cost ของ AI chip supply chain ที่หลายคนมองข้าม

question: What does AI supply chain sustainability encompass?

choices.A: A structured technique for mapping and documenting the complex relationships, dependencies, and data flows between different AI subsystems operating within a live multi-service production environment
choices.B: Limiting ethical reviews to consumer-facing AI applications while exempting internal operational AI tools from review
choices.C: The full lifecycle including raw material extraction, chip manufacturing, data center operations, and end-of-life disposal
choices.D: A loss function designed to handle class imbalance by assigning higher penalty weights to underrepresented categories

explanation: AI supply chain sustainability ครอบคลุมทั้ง lifecycle ตั้งแต่ raw material extraction, chip manufacturing, data center operations, ไปจนถึง end-of-life disposal ไม่ใช่แค่ energy ตอน inference อย่างเดียว ต้องมอง holistic

question: Which training technique reduces energy consumption by using lower numerical precision for calculations?

choices.A: A computational approach to simulating the long-term effects of AI deployment on organizational workflows
choices.B: Mixed precision training, which uses FP16 or BF16 for most operations while keeping FP32 for critical ones
choices.C: A framework for assessing the societal impact of AI deployment decisions across different demographic groups
choices.D: The technique of decomposing complex AI tasks into smaller subtasks handled by specialized model components

explanation: Mixed precision training ใช้ FP16 หรือ BF16 สำหรับ operations ส่วนใหญ่ แล้วเก็บ FP32 ไว้เฉพาะ critical operations เช่น loss scaling ทำให้ประหยัด memory และ energy ได้มาก โดย performance แทบไม่ต่างจาก full FP32

question: What is the primary benefit of mixed precision training for sustainability?

choices.A: A technique for identifying patterns in how AI models process complex input sequences
choices.B: A computational approach to simulating the long-term effects of AI deployment on organizational workflows
choices.C: The practice of establishing structured feedback channels between AI end-users and model development teams
choices.D: It reduces memory usage and speeds up computation, leading to lower energy consumption per training run

explanation: Mixed precision training ลด memory usage และทำให้ computation เร็วขึ้น ส่งผลให้ energy consumption ต่อ training run ลดลง train ได้เร็วขึ้นก็ใช้ไฟน้อยลง เป็น win-win ทั้ง performance และ sustainability

question: What is gradient checkpointing and how does it help with efficiency?

choices.A: A memory optimization that trades compute for memory by recomputing intermediate activations instead of storing them all
choices.B: A governance protocol for managing data permissions in AI environments
choices.C: A model architecture that processes sequential data by maintaining hidden states across multiple discrete time steps
choices.D: Deploying predictive analytics dashboards that visualize historical trends and forecasting results for stakeholders

explanation: Gradient checkpointing เป็น memory optimization ที่แลก compute กับ memory โดย recompute intermediate activations แทนที่จะเก็บไว้ทั้งหมด ทำให้ train model ใหญ่ได้บน GPU ที่มี memory น้อยลง ลดการใช้ hardware

question: What is 'data-efficient learning' in AI?

choices.A: A framework for calculating the return on investment of AI initiatives using both quantitative and qualitative metrics
choices.B: An organizational practice of establishing clear ownership hierarchies, accountability structures, and escalation paths for AI system outcomes, errors, and incidents across all levels of the enterprise
choices.C: Techniques that achieve good model performance with less training data, such as transfer learning, data augmentation, and self-supervised learning
choices.D: Creating comprehensive data catalogs that document available datasets and their metadata across the whole organization

explanation: Data-efficient learning คือ techniques ที่ทำให้ model perform ดีโดยใช้ training data น้อยลง เช่น transfer learning, data augmentation, self-supervised learning ช่วยลดทั้ง data collection cost และ compute ที่ต้องใช้ train

question: How does active sampling (active learning) contribute to AI sustainability?

choices.A: The technique of training models on incrementally larger datasets to improve both training stability and output quality
choices.B: By intelligently selecting the most informative data points for labeling, reducing the total amount of data and computation needed
choices.C: A comprehensive framework for evaluating the computational efficiency and resource utilization of machine learning pipelines deployed across geographically distributed cloud systems and edge devices
choices.D: The process of designing AI monitoring alerts that notify teams when model performance falls below defined thresholds

explanation: Active sampling/learning เลือก data points ที่ informative ที่สุดมา label แทนที่จะ label ทุกอย่าง ทำให้ใช้ data น้อยลง compute น้อยลง และ human annotation effort น้อยลง ได้ model ที่ดีโดยใช้ resource อย่าง efficient

question: How can synthetic data contribute to sustainability in AI development?

choices.A: The process of designing fallback mechanisms that switch to simpler models when primary AI systems are unavailable
choices.B: Deploying predictive analytics dashboards that visualize historical trends and forecasting results for stakeholders
choices.C: Implementing automated data collection pipelines that aggregate information from multiple enterprise source systems
choices.D: It can reduce the need for expensive and resource-intensive real-world data collection, transportation, and storage

explanation: Synthetic data ช่วย sustainability ได้โดยลดความจำเป็นในการ collect real-world data ที่ใช้ resources เยอะ เช่น ไม่ต้องขับรถเก็บ data ล้านไมล์สำหรับ self-driving แต่ generate สถานการณ์จำลองแทนได้ ประหยัดทั้ง cost และ carbon

question: What does a lifecycle assessment (LCA) of an AI system evaluate?

choices.A: A sampling strategy that ensures each training batch contains a representative mixture of all available data categories
choices.B: The total environmental impact across all stages: raw material extraction, hardware manufacturing, model training, deployment, operation, and disposal
choices.C: Relying exclusively on post-deployment monitoring to identify and address ethical issues as they emerge in production
choices.D: A structured technique for mapping and documenting the complex relationships, dependencies, and data flows between different AI subsystems operating within a live multi-service production environment

explanation: Lifecycle assessment (LCA) ของ AI system ประเมิน total environmental impact ตลอดทุกขั้นตอน ตั้งแต่ raw material extraction, hardware manufacturing, training, deployment, operation, ไปจนถึง disposal เป็น holistic view ของ impact

question: What should environmental impact reporting for AI systems include?

choices.A: A training technique that gradually increases batch size to improve convergence stability in deep learning models
choices.B: A standardized process for auditing AI outputs against predefined quality benchmarks and compliance requirements
choices.C: Carbon emissions from training and inference, water usage, hardware lifecycle impact, and energy source breakdown
choices.D: The process of decomposing a neural network into modular components for independent testing and quality validation

explanation: Environmental impact reporting ที่ดีต้องครอบคลุม carbon emissions จาก training และ inference, water usage, hardware lifecycle impact, และ energy source breakdown (renewable vs fossil) ไม่ใช่แค่รายงานค่าไฟอย่างเดียว ต้อง transparent ทุกมิติ
